domain: Extreme Football Throwdown (EFT) — competitive real-time possession sport (GMod gamemode)
state_space: Ball possession token × 10-player spatial manifold × per-player charge/knockdown status. Effective contest space collapses to ~1400-unit radius around ball at any moment.
dynamics_operator: Possession transfer via tackle (direct body collision at charge speed) or throw/receive. Charge state gates tackle outcome — uncharged attacker loses head-on; charged attacker wins.
perturbation_operator: Opponent tackle attempt, knockdown state, fumble on failed charge, ball reset on out-of-bounds.
stability_condition: No single player retains possession >~2 seconds before opponent contest (empirical 2-second possession turnover rule from match data). Game remains in continuous contest when this holds.
failure_mode: Possession monopoly collapse: one team achieves sustained uncontested possession, reducing opponent agency to zero and producing deterministic scoring. Also: dominant-strategy emergence when one mechanic (e.g. charge) is too low-cost.

observable_metrics.name: Possession duration
observable_metrics.estimator: Time between possession_gain and possession_loss per carrier

timescale_regime: Sub-second possession decisions (~0.5-2s per possession), 5-15 minute match duration, 60-300s per round.
non_geometric_elements: Charge state is binary with no spatial extent — it is a pure internal flag
Team assignment is discrete and fixed per round
Goal scoring is a discrete reset event — resets spatial state to neutral
Knockdown is a timed exclusion state with fixed recovery duration
edge_conditions: Ball reset to center on out-of-bounds resets possession contest without scoring
Overtime rule fires on tied score at time limit — prevents absorbing equilibrium
Map geometry creates local topological obstructions (tunnels, narrow corridors) that suppress throw coordination
Bot-only matches underestimate B3 (coordination) — human throw rate expected 2-3x higher
notes: Empirical data from 13 bot matches across 8 maps. Temple_sacrifice_v3 flagged as anomalous (bot suicide behavior: 755 respawns, only 11 tackles). Remaining 12 matches show stable contest dynamics. Tackle/possession ratio 2.5-3.5x; throw rate ~5-15% of possessions.
substrate_formalism: continuous spatial manifold with discrete possession token + discrete charge/knockdown states per agent
T1_notes: Possession changes occur faster than strategic reorganization — perturbation timescale << relaxation timescale

structural_bases.B1_note: Charge commitment is the primary basin boundary — entering charge is costly if mistimed (vulnerable to knockdown), creating a genuine energy threshold before possession contest
structural_bases.B2_note: State space is 10-dimensional (player positions) but collapses to proximity zone around ball. Expression bounded by spatial density around ball carrier.
structural_bases.B3_note: Coordination is LOW in observed bot data (~5-15% throw rate). Designed for higher human coordination but structurally functional at B3=low — game remains playable with near-zero passing.
structural_bases.B4_note: Decision stack is shallow: 1-2 read depth (does opponent have ball? Am I charged? Is path to goal clear?). No recursive planning required.

empirical_data.data_quality: PARTIAL — bot-only matches, no human baseline yet

metric_layer.distance_definition: 1 / avg_tackle_per_possession_ratio = fraction of tackles resulting in possession transfer (0.34 at ratio=2.98)

assumptions: Bot data underestimates coordination; human throw rate expected 20-30%

measurement_layer.distance_provenance: Derived from bot match replay data (12 valid matches, empirical ratio)
